feedingProfile.preferredNpk: balanced (e.g. 10-10-10 or 20-20-20) houseplant fertilizer, applied lightly

commonIssues: Root rot in overly moist or poorly drained soil — do not let the pot sit in water
Yellowing leaves from too much water or too little light
Brown, curled leaf tips from over-fertilizing
Spindly, stretched stems when light is too dark
Watch for aphids, spider mites, mealybugs, and scale
Toxic to humans and pets (calcium oxalate crystals) — keep out of reach
Avoid direct sunlight, which can burn the leaves; grow in bright indirect light

source.citation: North Carolina Extension Gardener Plant Toolbox — Philodendron hederaceum: prefers medium light, tolerates low light (deep shade <2h direct sun to partial shade 2-6h); keep soil slightly moist with good drainage, slow watering in winter; USDA zones 11a-12b; trailing or climbing tropical evergreen vine; toxic (calcium oxalate)

Missouri Botanical Garden Plant Finder — Philodendron hederaceum: winter hardy USDA zones 11-12; part shade, bright indirect light indoors, avoid direct sun; medium water, reduce fall to late winter; soil-based potting mix; root rot in overly moist soils; aphids, spider mites, mealybugs, scale; easy culture, trains on trellis or trails from hanging basket

UF/IFAS Gardening Solutions — Heartleaf Philodendron: water when top inch of soil is dry; bright diffuse light, avoid direct sun; temperatures below 50°F (10°C) are too cool; fertilize every 3-4 months (light feeder; excess fertilizer browns/curls leaf tips); lightweight well-drained potting media; suits hanging baskets and containers

Iowa State University Extension, Growing Philodendrons at Home — water when top of soil is dry, evenly moist but not wet; fertilize lightly once or twice a month during spring/summer active growth with balanced all-purpose fertilizer; home temperatures 65-80°F (18-27°C) well tolerated; grows best slightly pot-bound, repot one size up when overcrowded

Iowa State University Extension, Growing Philodendrons at Home — pot-size guidance: container large enough to support the top growth without toppling; does well slightly pot-bound; repot one size larger when overcrowded

Confidence note: sources give qualitative triggers, not fixed numbers. baseIntervalDays=7 derives 'water when the top inch of soil is dry' (UF/IFAS, Iowa State) to a typical indoor cadence. recommendedMinLiters=2 maps the standard 15 cm (6 in) starter pot / hanging basket implied by extension guidance to liters; no source states liters. heatStressC=35 is a conservative estimate above the 18-27°C comfortable range (Iowa State); feeding interval of 30 days follows Iowa State (monthly in growing season) over UF/IFAS's every-3-4-months — both are within the lightly-fed guidance.
